prevalence.population: Women of reproductive age in Brazil
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with obesity and insulin resistance prevalence

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to endocrinology/gynecology
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rotterdam criteria: 2 of 3 (oligo/anovulation, hyperandrogenism clinical/biochemical, polycystic ovaries on ultrasound) after exclusion of other causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improved with weight management and access to hormonal/metabolic therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Ultrasound and hormone labs availability varies; APS often initiates evaluation and referral
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle/weight loss, combined oral contraceptives for cycle control, metformin for insulin resistance, fertility management in specialist care; screen for metabolic risk
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Obesity prevalence, limited access to specialized fertility services in SUS, and inconsistent metabolic screening
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of metformin and primary care chronic disease management
